title: Rei II and Rei I
wikitext:
[]: Rei II and Rei I             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Sun Oct 23, 2005 2:12 pm We know that Rei I is the soul of Zerogouki, no? And Rei II pilots Zerogouki. But here is my question. Rei's soul is that of Lillith and her body of Yui and Lillith. It is said that only one clone of Lillith can exist at a time because Lillith only has one soul. An Eva needs a soul to operate and Zerogouki has Rei I's soul. But Rei I and Rei II have the same soul, Lillith's. And can't a soul only exist in one place at one time? So doesn't this mean the Zerogouki can't have Rei I's soul? Because Rei II and Rei I have the same soul. Not to imply the Nagko is Zerogouki's soul because it cannot be. We all know that but this evidence semes to point that it cannot be Rei I's soul either. And we already know from the show that a soul is diffrent from one's personality. (IE: Shogouki does not act like Yui) So has anyone else noticed this?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 23, 2005 4:14 pm Rei manages to get around these restrictions. Remember the very first time we see her (outside the OP) - a brief glimpse of her in school uniform, when we know she's lying injured in NERV HQ. She manages to cheat the restriction of only being in one place at a time.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Oct 23, 2005 5:08 pm Rei I is most certainly within Unit-00, but as to the status of her soul within it, this is still an open question. Some have postulated that Lilith's soul(Rei's) has the ability to be in several places at one, both in time and in space. Others have suggested that Rei's soul has been split somehow, and a piece of it left behind in Unit-00. And of course some have proposed another soul entirely, Naoko's, within Unit-00 to try to resolve the apparent paradox. It's worth noting that in episode #13, Unit-00 remotely generates an AT Field, which would indicate that the presence inside, Rei I, must have the ability to do this. Under the general consensus, Evangelion's must have a soul to generate an AT field. So this points strongly to the fact that Rei I, if not fully Lilith's soul, is very "soul like". A breakoff piece of Lilith's soul? Might this somehow explain the apparent difference in the personalities of the Rei I and II? A point to remember in all this is that Angel's souls are quite likely of a much greater magnitude than human souls. Thus, even a small portion of Lilith's soul might be enough for an Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by akda1ndaonly1 » Sun Oct 23, 2005 10:57 pm I think a good example of how souls can be shared/split is how Israfel splits into two, which probably means that it shares its soul with the other half. But they both still function normally even without having a complete soul.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Oct 23, 2005 11:42 pm This is own of those things I feel you should dwell on when iot comes to EVA. Ponder it a bit, but move on and it touches on thee nature of souls in this show somthing not full explaned. Its better to concintrate on the main plot. DD

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Oct 25, 2005 12:04 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Some have postulated that Lilith's soul(Rei's) has the ability to be in several places at one, both in time and in space. Others have suggested that Rei's soul has been split somehow, and a piece of it left behind in Unit-00. And of course some have proposed another soul entirely, Naoko's, within Unit-00 to try to resolve the apparent paradox.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Oct 25, 2005 8:01 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Rei I is most certainly within Unit-00, but as to the status of her soul within it, this is still an open question. Some have postulated that Lilith's soul(Rei's) has the ability to be in several places at one, both in time and in space. Others have suggested that Rei's soul has been split somehow, and a piece of it left behind in Unit-00. And of course some have proposed another soul entirely, Naoko's, within Unit-00 to try to resolve the apparent paradox.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Tue Oct 25, 2005 12:08 pm Shin-seiki wrote: . It's worth noting that in episode #13, Unit-00 remotely generates an AT Field, which would indicate that the presence inside, Rei I, must have the ability to do this.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Oct 25, 2005 1:34 pm Yes. That's the one. It happens when the team connects the pilot to Unit-00, via the dummy bodies. The team says somthing about generating a field of 2 yocto(10^-24). Evidently quite small in relation to whatever the basic unit of AT Fields is measured in, but enough to seemingly rouse Iruel into activity. Actually, in both the ADV Platinum and LTP scripts, it's simply mentioned that the field is simply generated at 2 yocto. No unit is given. Is the AT Field strength a dimensionless number? Shin-seiki wrote: I would say that might apply to Sources of Life, but Yui in Shogouki demonstrates that her human soul is more than equal in power to every Angel she takes on...

[]:             Quote Post by wrack » Tue Oct 25, 2005 4:22 pm w00t. Time for random, rabid speculation again. After thinking this over for, like, five minutes, I'm starting to somewhat believe that Lilith's soul was indeed somehow split for Unit 00 and Rei 2. I'm basing this on the gals' varying opinions on a certain person. You see, Rei 2 thought Gendo as some sorta father figure, while Unit 00 thought, or 'thought,' that Gendo sucks and must diediediedie, these thoughts probaly caused by the fella letting her get killed or something. Later, a huge-ass explosion takes place, during which the soul perhaps maybe somewhat gets merged as one again. Nerv extracts the now again full Lilith soul, personalities merged and all, and what they get as a result is a Rei with certain despise towards the commander. Of course it could be just two copies of the soul put together but that would make even less sense. So yeah. Rambling, rambling.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Oct 25, 2005 7:01 pm wrack wrote: w00t. Time for random, rabid speculation again. After thinking this over for, like, five minutes, I'm starting to somewhat believe that Lilith's soul was indeed somehow split for Unit 00 and Rei 2.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Oct 25, 2005 11:51 pm Why so much emphasis in soul division and not a word on soul copying?

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Oct 26, 2005 5:38 am where does everyone get these names for the evas from!? this has really been bugging me.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Oct 26, 2005 5:55 am I think it's important to remember that we see Rei/Lilith doing a whole lot of stuff that's impossible for other people. Compared to the whole "growing to several hundred miles tall naked woman" and the repeated Phantom Rei phenomena, I don't think having a somewhat stretchy soul is too much to worry about. ice reaper wrote: where does everyone get these names for the evas from!? this has really been bugging me.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Oct 26, 2005 8:19 am ice reaper wrote: where does everyone get these names for the evas from!? this has really been bugging me.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Oct 26, 2005 9:38 am ; _ ; i am! i am a dubbie! i beg your forgiveness.... thanks

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Wed Oct 26, 2005 10:57 am ^ No need to pardon for forgiveness for your own personal preference on how to watch the show. 8) I have seen the entirity of Evangelion both subbed and subtitled and I have come to prefer the dub. The way I see it, I'm watching it the same way the creators and the Japanese audience did: In their own native language.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Oct 26, 2005 12:14 pm Heidroch wrote: ^ No need to pardon for forgiveness for your own personal preference on how to watch the show. 8) I have seen the entirity of Evangelion both subbed and subtitled and I have come to prefer the dub. The way I see it, I'm watching it the same way the creators and the Japanese audience did: In their own native language.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Oct 26, 2005 3:08 pm Shin-seiki wrote: ice reaper wrote: where does everyone get these names for the evas from!? this has really been bugging me.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Wed Oct 26, 2005 4:46 pm I would too. I'm thinking of making a Fan fiction... But, let's get back on topic? I want to read what ya'll think about Zerogouki's soul.
